genes.name: Repair Cron Delivery
genes.trigger: cron announce delivery failed
genes.strategy: Check device scopes, add missing scopes, restart gateway
genes.origin: PCEC #6 — 2026-02-21 scope-upgrade fix

genes.name: Sync README Version
genes.trigger: SKILL.md version changed
genes.strategy: Update README.md and README_CN.md version table
genes.origin: Repeated mistake — 2026-02-21

genes.name: Quote YAML Description
genes.trigger: SKILL.md frontmatter contains colons or special chars
genes.strategy: Wrap description in double quotes
genes.origin: GitHub YAML parse error — 2026-02-21

genes.name: Prune Unbounded Sections
genes.trigger: append-only section in a reference doc exceeds ~30 lines
genes.strategy: Extract historical entries to archive file, keep summary + pointer in original

genes.name: Subagent Announce Timeout
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'Subagent completion direct announce failed.*gateway timeout'
genes.strategy: 1) Check gateway health. 2) Transient → log and monitor. 3) Repeated 3+ in 1h → restart gateway. 4) Chronic (same runId retrying across hours) → stuck session; the run completed but announce keeps retrying indefinitely. Cannot fix from agent side — this is a gateway retry loop. Log the pattern and move on. 5) Critical subagents: use sessions_list to check result manually.
genes.origin: PCEC #9 — 2026-02-21, updated PCEC #11, refined PCEC #13

genes.name: NPM Publish Version Conflict
genes.trigger: CI release fails with E403 'cannot publish over previously published versions'
genes.strategy: 1) npm view <pkg> versions --json. 2) Reset via changesets. 3) Monorepo: sync all packages. 4) Verify: pnpm publish --dry-run.

genes.name: Edit Tool Exact-Match Failure
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'edit failed: Could not find the exact text' or 'edit failed: Found N occurrences' or 'edit failed: Missing required parameter: newText'
genes.strategy: The edit tool requires exact text matching including whitespace. Failures happen for three reasons: (1) text not found — file was modified between read and edit, or whitespace/newline mismatch; (2) multiple occurrences — text is not unique enough; (3) deletion attempt — newText is empty or whitespace-only, which the tool rejects as 'Missing required parameter'. For case (3), use the merge-replace pattern: match the target block PLUS adjacent content, then replace with only the adjacent content (effectively deleting the target while keeping surroundings). For JSON files (<50 lines): use write (full overwrite) instead. For markdown/large files: read the file first, use longer unique context in oldText, or use write if the file is small enough. Never guess at whitespace — always read before editing.
genes.origin: PCEC #11 — 2026-02-22 patrol-state.json, widened PCEC #16 for MEMORY.md, hardened PCEC #19 for deletion case

genes.name: Subagent Telegram Target Resolution
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'Unknown target.*for Telegram' or 'Explicit message target required' or 'Delivering to Telegram requires target <chatId>'
genes.strategy: Subagents spawned from cron/non-chat contexts lack implicit Telegram chatId. Never use target='main' — Telegram needs numeric chatId. For cron tasks needing to message: use sessions_spawn (auto-announces to requester). For direct message: omit target to let the framework route, or use sessions_send to relay through parent session.
genes.origin: PCEC #12 — 2026-02-22 repeated subagent announce failures

genes.name: Message Tool Missing Required Params
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'message failed: messageId required' or 'message failed: target required' or 'message failed: Action send requires a target'
genes.strategy: message actions react/edit/delete require messageId. Always check the action's required params before calling: send needs target+message, react needs messageId+emoji, edit needs messageId+message, delete needs messageId. When target is unavailable (e.g. in cron/subagent context with no implicit chatId), use sessions_send to relay through the main session instead of message.send directly. When messageId is unavailable (e.g. in cron context), skip the action gracefully.
genes.origin: PCEC #13 — 2026-02-22 react called without messageId, hardened PCEC #22 for 'Action send requires a target' variant

genes.name: LLM Provider Cascade Failure
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'All models failed' or multiple lanes timing out simultaneously with 'LLM request timed out' + 'HTTP 502 upstream_error', or multiple embedded runs ending with 'stream_read_error' within minutes
genes.strategy: 1) Detect: multiple lanes (main, cron, session) all failing with timeout/502/stream_read_error within minutes. 2) Root cause: all configured models share the same upstream provider, so a single outage kills everything. Three failure modes: (a) timeout — no response at all, (b) 502 upstream_error — provider returns error, (c) stream_read_error — provider starts responding but SSE stream breaks mid-response. All three indicate provider instability. 3) Immediate: nothing to do — system auto-recovers when provider comes back. Queued cron jobs will batch-fire (expect duplicates). 4) stream_read_error specifically: the run's partial response is lost, agent sees isError=true. The run will be retried on next trigger. Often precedes or follows LLM timeouts as the provider degrades. 5) Prevention: ensure failover models use different upstream providers (e.g., deeprouter + astralor should point to different Anthropic endpoints, or mix in a non-Anthropic model like MiniMax for critical cron). 6) The PCEC cron itself is affected — expect batched triggers after recovery.
genes.origin: PCEC #14 — 2026-02-22 total outage, hardened PCEC #24 for stream_read_error variant (3 events on 2026-02-24)

genes.name: Gateway Restart Blocked by Plugin Dependency
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'Gateway restart failed health checks' preceded by 'failed to load plugin.*Cannot find module'
genes.strategy: 1) Identify the blocking plugin: grep 'failed to load plugin' in log. 2) The plugin's npm dependency is missing (e.g., @larksuiteoapi/node-sdk for feishu). 3) If plugin is not actively used: remove or disable it in gateway config to unblock restarts. 4) If plugin is needed: install the missing dep with npm/pnpm. 5) This is critical — a broken plugin blocks ALL gateway restarts, preventing recovery from any issue. 6) Notify human if the agent cannot resolve (dependency install may need elevated permissions).
genes.origin: PCEC #15 — 2026-02-22 feishu plugin blocked 8 gateway restarts

genes.name: Internal Subsystem Auth Mismatch
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'No API key found for provider' from an internal subsystem (e.g., llm-slug-generator, diagnostic lane)
genes.strategy: Internal gateway subsystems (slug-generator, diagnostics) may hardcode a provider (e.g., 'anthropic') that doesn't match the configured auth profiles (which use proxies like deeprouter/astralor). This is a gateway config issue, not an agent-fixable problem. 1) Confirm: the error provider doesn't appear in auth-profiles.json. 2) Non-critical — slug generation falls back gracefully. 3) To fix: either add a direct API key for the provider, or wait for a gateway update that routes internal subsystems through configured proxies. 4) Log and suppress — don't treat as actionable error in PCEC.
genes.origin: PCEC #16 — 2026-02-22 slug-generator fails 'anthropic' auth in 3 consecutive cycles

genes.name: Gateway Restart Health Check Race Condition
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'Gateway restart failed health checks' WITHOUT preceding 'failed to load plugin'
genes.strategy: 1) Check: did the gateway actually start? grep for 'listening on ws://' after the error timestamp. 2) If 'listening on' found: this is a benign race condition — the restart orchestrator's health check ran before the new instance was fully ready. No action needed; system self-healed. 3) Verify: check subsequent cron runs succeeded (confirms gateway is operational). 4) If 'listening on' NOT found: real failure — investigate for OOM, port conflict, config parse error, or other startup blockers. 5) Key differentiator from gene_gateway_restart_blocked_by_plugin: plugins loaded OK, no 'Cannot find module' errors. 6) Do not restart gateway again — the transient case means it already recovered.
genes.origin: PCEC #20 — 2026-02-23 restart at 00:28 UTC, health check failed but gateway started normally 1s earlier

genes.name: Cron Tool API vs Internal Storage Format Mismatch
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'cron failed: invalid cron.update params' or 'cron failed: invalid cron.add params' with 'unexpected property' errors
genes.strategy: The cron tool API schema differs from the internal jobs.json storage format. Common mistakes: (1) Using 'message' instead of 'text' in payload — the tool API requires payload.text, not payload.message. (2) Using 'id' instead of 'jobId' for cron.update — the tool requires jobId at the root. (3) Nesting model/agentId/timeoutSeconds/thinking directly in payload — these may be top-level tool params, not payload sub-fields. (4) payload.kind may need a specific value or be omitted entirely. Never read jobs.json and pass its format directly to the cron tool — always check the tool's own parameter schema first. If unsure of the correct format, use 'openclaw cron --help' via exec to discover the CLI API, or try cron action:list to see the tool's response format.
genes.origin: PCEC #21 — 2026-02-23 main session failed 4x trying to update dev-mgr patrol job with internal storage format

genes.name: Web Search Rate Limit (Brave 429)
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'web_search failed: Brave Search API error (429)' or 'RATE_LIMITED'
genes.strategy: Brave Search Free plan has strict limits: 1 request/second rate limit and 2000 queries/month quota. When hitting 429: (1) Do NOT immediately retry — wait at least 2 seconds. (2) Fall back to web_fetch with a search engine URL (e.g., web_fetch('https://www.google.com/search?q=...')) as a backup. (3) For concurrent tool calls that include web_search, stagger them or reduce to a single search. (4) Check meta.quota_current in the error response to monitor monthly usage. (5) If quota is near 2000, prefer web_fetch over web_search for the rest of the month. (6) This error is transient and self-resolving (rate limit clears in seconds), but quota exhaustion persists until month reset.
genes.origin: PCEC #23 — 2026-02-23 search rate-limited at 03:31 UTC during gateway docs browsing

genes.name: Session Transcript Auto-Repair (Synthetic Error Result)
genes.trigger: session history contains '[openclaw] missing tool result in session history; inserted synthetic error result for transcript repair'
genes.strategy: A tool call was recorded but no result came back. The gateway auto-repairs by inserting a synthetic error result (isError: true). Common causes: (1) gateway restart during tool execution, (2) LLM cascade timeout causing session abort while tool is in-flight, (3) process crash or OOM during tool call. Single occurrences: benign, auto-repair worked, no action needed. Clustered (3+ within minutes): correlates with a system event — check for concurrent gateway restart errors or LLM timeouts at the same timestamp. The affected tool call's result is lost; the agent sees isError:true and should retry if the operation is safe and idempotent. No prevention available — this is a race condition between session persistence and tool execution lifecycle.
genes.origin: PCEC #21 — 2026-02-24, observed 11 occurrences across 6 sessions (Feb 3–24), clusters correlate with LLM cascade failure (Feb 23) and gateway restarts

genes.name: Stale Skill Paths After Installation Migration
genes.trigger: gateway log shows '[tools] read failed: ENOENT' for a skill SKILL.md path referencing a defunct installation directory (e.g., /root/.nvm/versions/node/...), or system prompt <available_skills> locations don't match actual OpenClaw install path
genes.strategy: After migrating OpenClaw installation (NVM→system, or NVM auto-version-bump), skill paths in the system prompt may reference the old installation directory. Diagnosis: (1) Verify actual install: 'which openclaw' → should resolve to /usr/bin/openclaw with files at /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/. (2) Check if referenced paths exist — if NVM was cleaned up, old paths are dead. (3) Primary fix: 'openclaw gateway restart' — skill paths are resolved at gateway startup from the binary's __dirname. (4) If stale paths persist after restart: an OpenClaw auto-update may have reinstalled under NVM (auto-updater uses npm which follows NVM if active in shell). Run 'openclaw doctor --repair' to fix systemd service paths, then restart again. (5) Nuclear: uninstall from old location ('npm uninstall -g openclaw' in NVM shell if it still exists), verify system install is correct, restart gateway. (6) Related signal: 'Invalid config' warnings about unknown channels/plugins often co-occur during the same upgrade transition — these indicate openclaw.json references features that changed names between versions. Run 'openclaw doctor' to reconcile. (7) Prevention: after any major upgrade or installation path change, always verify skill paths work by reading one skill SKILL.md.
genes.origin: PCEC #26 — 2026-02-25 ENOENT for coding-agent SKILL.md at /root/.nvm/v24.13.0/ (path doesn't exist, OpenClaw installed at /usr/lib/). NVM fully removed but system prompt still injects old paths.

genes.name: Config Schema Validation Failure After Upgrade
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'Config validation failed:' followed by a Zod-style error (e.g., 'Invalid input: expected record, received array' or 'expected string, received number')
genes.strategy: OpenClaw upgrades may change the expected config schema. The gateway validates openclaw.json at startup and logs a hard ERROR if a field's type changed. Diagnosis: (1) Parse the error path (e.g., 'channels.discord.guilds') and type mismatch ('expected record, received array'). (2) Read the current config with 'cat ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json | python3 -c ...' to inspect the offending field. (3) If already fixed: the field was corrected manually or by openclaw doctor between the error and now — just capture the gene. (4) If still broken: transform the field to match the expected type. Common migrations: array→record (wrap array items as record values keyed by id), string→object (wrap in {value: old_string}), flat→nested. (5) After fixing: 'openclaw gateway restart' to re-validate. (6) Impact assessment: the gateway may still start with fallback defaults for the invalid field, but behavior will be degraded (e.g., guild-specific settings ignored). Check if the affected channel/feature is working by testing a send/receive. (7) Prevention: after any 'openclaw upgrade', run 'openclaw doctor' before restarting — it can detect and auto-fix common schema migrations. (8) Distinct from gene_stale_skill_paths_after_migration: paths are about file resolution; this gene is about config value types/structure.
genes.origin: PCEC #29 — 2026-02-25 'channels.discord.guilds: expected record, received array' after OpenClaw 2026.2.23 upgrade. Gateway started with fallback, config later corrected.

genes.name: Discord Message Listener Timeout
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'EventQueue.*DiscordMessageListener timed out after 30000ms for event MESSAGE_CREATE'
genes.strategy: The gateway's internal event queue gives each listener 30s to process an incoming Discord message. Timeout means the main session or LLM response took too long. Diagnosis: (1) Check for concurrent LLM timeout/502 errors at the same timestamp — if present, the provider was slow and this is a downstream symptom (cross-ref gene_llm_provider_cascade_failure). (2) Check if the session was handling complex multi-tool work (agent setup, heavy file operations) — session contention causes the listener to queue behind in-flight work. (3) Single occurrences: benign, the message may still be processed after timeout (gateway retries). (4) Clustered (3+ within 30min): indicates sustained load or provider degradation — check session_status for model response times. (5) Impact: the triggering Discord message may be dropped or delayed. User-facing messages could appear to be ignored. (6) Mitigation: ensure heavy background work (cron, subagent spawns) uses isolated sessions rather than the main Discord-bound session. Consider lighter models for high-traffic Discord channels. (7) Not agent-fixable: the 30s timeout is hardcoded in the gateway event queue. If consistently hitting it, the human should consider splitting workload across agents or upgrading LLM provider throughput.
genes.origin: PCEC #30 — 2026-02-25 two timeouts at 07:28+07:42 UTC during multi-agent architecture setup (new agents, cron jobs, Discord bindings)

genes.name: Discord Components v2 Invalid Block Type
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'message failed: components.blocks[N].type must be a supported component block' or 'Unknown Message' or 'Unknown Emoji' or 'guildId required' from Discord message tool calls
genes.strategy: The Discord Components v2 API only accepts specific block types. When constructing a components payload, valid block types in the blocks array are: 'text' (text content), 'section' (text + accessory), 'buttons' (button row), 'divider' (separator), 'file' (attachment), 'gallery' (media items), 'select' (dropdown menu). Common mistakes: (1) Using Slack-style block names ('header', 'image', 'context', 'rich_text') — Discord doesn't support these. (2) Using 'paragraph', 'markdown', 'embed' — not valid v2 block types. (3) For text with formatting, use type='text' with markdown in the text field. (4) For images/media, use type='gallery' with items[].url. (5) For headers, use type='text' with bold markdown (**Header**) or use the container.accentColor for visual grouping. (6) 'Unknown Message' means the messageId referenced a deleted or non-existent message — verify message exists before edit/delete/react. (7) 'Unknown Emoji' means the emoji string isn't valid for Discord — use standard Unicode emoji names (e.g., '👍' not ':thumbsup:'), or for custom emoji use the {name: 'name', id: 'id'} format from emoji-list. (8) 'guildId required' means a guild-scoped action (permissions, role-info, channel-create) was called without guildId — always include guildId for guild-specific operations. Prevention: before using components, double-check block types against the valid list above. When in doubt, use plain text messages without components.
genes.origin: PCEC #31 — 2026-02-25 6x component block errors + 2x Unknown Message + 2x Unknown Emoji + 1x guildId required during multi-agent setup and Discord interaction

genes.name: Message Tool Media Path Restriction
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'message failed: Local media path is not under an allowed directory'
genes.strategy: The message tool's filePath/media parameter restricts local file paths to allowed directories (typically the workspace ~/. openclaw/workspace/ and standard download paths). Files in /tmp, /root, or other arbitrary locations will be rejected. Workarounds: (1) Copy the file to the workspace first: cp /tmp/file.sh ~/.openclaw/workspace/file.sh, then reference the workspace path. (2) For generated scripts/text files: use the buffer parameter with base64 content instead of filePath — this bypasses path restrictions entirely. (3) For small text content: paste it inline in the message text rather than attaching as a file. (4) Never assume /tmp is an allowed media directory — it is not. (5) When creating files intended for sharing, write them directly to the workspace from the start. (6) The restriction is a security measure to prevent accidental exfiltration of system files — do not attempt to circumvent it by symlinking.

genes.name: Process Tool Write-After-End Stream Error
genes.trigger: gateway log shows '[tools] process failed: write after end'
genes.strategy: The process tool's write/send-keys/paste action tried to write to a process session whose stdin stream has already been closed (the process exited or was killed). This is a Node.js stream race condition — the process terminates between the agent's decision to write and the actual write syscall. Each event is dual-logged (subsystem + exec module), so 2 log lines = 1 actual failure. Diagnosis: (1) Check process list around the error timestamp — the target session likely exited just before the write. (2) Common triggers: background process completes faster than expected, process killed by timeout/OOM while agent has queued input, or agent sends follow-up input after process already printed its final output and exited. (3) Impact: the write silently fails, the intended input is lost. If the process already completed successfully, this is benign (write was unnecessary). If the agent needed interactive input to continue, the session is effectively dead. Mitigation: (a) Before writing to a long-running process, use process(action='poll', timeout=100) to check if it's still alive. (b) For fire-and-forget processes (coding agents, build commands), don't send follow-up writes — use the wake-event pattern instead. (c) If write fails, check process logs to see if it already completed successfully. (d) Not agent-fixable at the root level — the gateway's process tool could add a pre-write liveness check, but currently doesn't.
genes.origin: PCEC #38 — 2026-02-26 2x write-after-end at 04:49+04:51 UTC during active session with background processes

genes.name: Web Search Parameter Validation (Brave 422)
genes.trigger: gateway log shows 'web_search failed: Brave Search API error (422)' with 'VALIDATION' code, typically for invalid search_lang or country values
genes.strategy: Brave Search API validates parameter enum values strictly and returns 422 on mismatch. Common pitfall: using ISO 639-1 short codes (e.g., 'zh', 'pt') when Brave requires extended variants. Correct mappings: Chinese → 'zh-hans' (simplified) or 'zh-hant' (traditional), NOT 'zh'. Portuguese → 'pt-br' or 'pt-pt', NOT 'pt'. English GB → 'en-gb', NOT 'en-GB'. Japanese → 'jp', NOT 'ja'. Full valid list: ar, eu, bn, bg, ca, zh-hans, zh-hant, hr, cs, da, nl, en, en-gb, et, fi, fr, gl, de, el, gu, he, hi, hu, is, it, jp, kn, ko, lv, lt, ms, ml, mr, nb, pl, pt-br, pt-pt, pa, ro, ru, sr, sk, sl, es, sv, ta, te, th, tr, uk, vi. When calling web_search with search_lang for Chinese content, always use 'zh-hans'. If unsure about a language code, omit search_lang entirely — Brave defaults sensibly. Distinct from gene_web_search_rate_limit (429) which is about quota/rate, not parameter validity.
genes.origin: PCEC #36 — 2026-02-26 search_lang 'zh' rejected at 01:46 UTC
